title: Complete OAuth callback into customer session
verify: Validated provider identity resolves or creates exactly one PostgreSQL-backed Medusa customer and establishes customer session.
Repeat provider identity reuses customer; same-email different-provider collision creates no link or session.
Callback redirects only to configured storefront completion path and strips provider query/token data.
Start/completion rate limits are bounded and sanitized; failure leaves no partial session/customer linkage.

verify.reason: Independent T3 verification found incomplete first-login rollback on session failure and a false-pass default integration dispatcher.
Adversarial T3 re-verification found callback cleanup race and uncompensated post-create identity-read failure.
T3 functional and semantic verification passed after final bounded retry 2/2.

evidence_required: Collision, duplicate callback, redirect, rate-limit, and partial-failure assertions.
HUMAN_CHECKPOINT and rollback/recovery evidence required by T3 policy.
constraints: Use Medusa customer-account workflow and Auth/Customer modules; no direct core table writes.
Establish session server-side before fixed redirect.
Rate limiter stores only bounded hashed client keys and coarse counters.
invariants: One provider identity resolves at most one customer actor.
purpose: Convert validated Google/VK identities into one durable Medusa customer and safe storefront session.
success_outcome: Backend callback completion is replay-safe, collision-safe, session-establishing, rate-limited, and fixed-redirect only.
anti_goals: Do not implement storefront UI or cart merge.
Do not auto-link accounts by email.
Do not modify Medusa Core or write auth tables directly.

stop_conditions: Supported Medusa workflow cannot create/link customer without direct core writes.
Session cannot be established without exposing a token to browser URL/storage.
Callback destination or account collision policy is ambiguous.
